2026-04-23T15:22:10.0608676Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T15:22:10.0609205Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T15:22:10.0609625Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T15:22:10.0609995Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T15:22:10.0610479Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T15:22:10.0610718Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T15:22:10.0610885Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T15:22:10.0611074Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T15:22:10.0611247Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T15:22:10.0611481Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-23T15:22:10.0611684Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T15:22:10.0611970Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T15:22:10.0612229Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

The README is largely a placeholder — the "Examples" section has no code samples, and the sample test files in `tests/Samples/` contain rich working examples that are not wired up with `#region Snippet:` tags for README injection.

The CHANGELOG now records the `1.0.0` stable release, but the README still lacks concrete code examples and meaningful Key concepts content suitable for a GA library.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **Examples section is empty of actual code** — the README only links to `https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples` with no inline examples showing how to create, list, get, or delete a pool\n- **Sample test files are not wired to the README** — `tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs`, and `tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs` contain full working code examples but use no `#region Snippet:` tags, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject them into the README\n- **Key concepts section is generic** — it only links to generic Azure SDK docs and does not explain service-specific concepts such as `DevOpsPoolResource`, `DevOpsVmssFabricProfile`, `DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile`, or `DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile`\n- **Authenticate the client section has no code snippet** — it only links to a quickstart guide rather than showing a short `ArmClient` + `DefaultAzureCredential` instantiation example\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section existing text:\n\n```markdown\nTo create an authenticated client and start interacting with Microsoft Azure resources, please install the [`Azure.Identity`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) package:\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Azure.Identity\n```\n\nThen instantiate an `ArmClient`:\n\n```C# Snippet:CreateDevOpsInfrastructureClient\nTokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\nArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\n```\n```\n\n2. **Key concepts** — replace the single generic link with a section that describes the main types:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\n### DevOpsPoolResource\nRepresents a Managed DevOps Pool — a pool of Azure virtual machines used as Azure DevOps agents. Each pool is tied to a resource group and an Azure DevOps organization.\n\n### DevOpsVmssFabricProfile\nDescribes the underlying Azure VMSS-based infrastructure for the pool, including VM SKU, OS images, network settings, and OS secrets management.\n\n### DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile\nAssociates the pool with one or more Azure DevOps organizations and projects.\n\n### DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile / DevOpsStatefulAgentProfile\nConfigures whether agents are stateless (fresh VM per job) or stateful (VM reused across jobs).\n\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html).\n```\n\n3. **Examples** — replace the generic link with snippet placeholders:\n\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\n### Create a Managed DevOps Pool\n\n```C# Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool\n```\n\n### Get a Managed DevOps Pool\n\n```C# Snippet:GetDevOpsPool\n```\n\n### List all Managed DevOps Pools in a resource group\n\n```C# Snippet:ListDevOpsPools\n```\n\n### Delete a Managed DevOps Pool\n\n```C# Snippet:DeleteDevOpsPool\n```\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nAdd `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` tags to the existing sample files so `Update-Snippets.ps1` can inject code into the README placeholders.\n\n#### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`\n\nIn the `CreateOrUpdate_PoolsCreateOrUpdate` method body, wrap the meaningful portion (after the client/resource group boilerplate) with:\n\n```csharp\n#region Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool\n// ... ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\n    ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\n    DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools();\n\n    #region Snippet:GetDevOpsPool\n    string poolName = \"pool\";\n    DevOpsPoolResource result = await collection.GetAsync(poolName);\n    Console.WriteLine($\"Pool name: {result.Data.Name}\");\n    #endregion\n}\n```\n\nAdd a new `List_PoolsList` 2026-04-23T15:26:31.1939908Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1940102Z **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure`
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1940387Z **Service directory:** `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/`
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1940529Z **Triggered by:** commit 601109e01 (PR #58580) by @azure-sdk
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1940543Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1940753Z ## What Changed
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1940759Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1941856Z The package version was incremented to `1.1.0-beta.1` following the first stable `1.0.0` release of `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` (Managed DevOps Pools management library). The CHANGELOG now records the `1.0.0` stable release, but the README still lacks concrete code examples and meaningful Key concepts content suitable for a GA library.
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1941862Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1941948Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1941952Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1942664Z - **Examples section is empty of actual code** — the README only links to `https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples` with no inline examples showing how to create, list, get, or delete a pool
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1944302Z - **Sample test files are not wired to the README** — `tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs`, and `tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs` contain full working code examples but use no `#region Snippet:` tags, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject them into the README
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1945369Z - **Key concepts section is generic** — it only links to generic Azure SDK docs and does not explain service-specific concepts such as `DevOpsPoolResource`, `DevOpsVmssFabricProfile`, `DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile`, or `DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile`
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1946013Z - **Authenticate the client section has no code snippet** — it only links to a quickstart guide rather than showing a short `ArmClient` + `DefaultAzureCredential` instantiation example
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1946020Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1946100Z <details>
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1946318Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1946323Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1946641Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1946654Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1946745Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1946751Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1947097Z #### `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` — Edit
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1947102Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1947533Z 1. **Authenticate the client** — replace the single-sentence link with a short snippet placeholder after the existing text:
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1947538Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1947616Z ```markdown
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1948312Z To create an authenticated client and start interacting with Microsoft Azure resources, please install the [`Azure.Identity`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) package:
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1948316Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1948393Z ```dotnetcli
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1948490Z dotnet add package Azure.Identity
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1948565Z ```
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1948569Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1948661Z Then instantiate an `ArmClient`:
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1948672Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1948808Z ```C# Snippet:CreateDevOpsInfrastructureClient
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1948950Z TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1949051Z ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1949125Z ```
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1949194Z ```
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1949198Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1949534Z 2. **Key concepts** — replace the single generic link with a section that describes the main types:
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1949538Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1949610Z ```markdown
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1949692Z ## Key concepts
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1949697Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1949781Z ### DevOpsPoolResource
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1950372Z Represents a Managed DevOps Pool — a pool of Azure virtual machines used as Azure DevOps agents. Each pool is tied to a resource group and an Azure DevOps organization.
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1950378Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1950469Z ### DevOpsVmssFabricProfile
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1951032Z Describes the underlying Azure VMSS-based infrastructure for the pool, including VM SKU, OS images, network settings, and OS secrets management.
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1951042Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1951145Z ### DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1951353Z Associates the pool with one or more Azure DevOps organizations and projects.
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1951357Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1951519Z ### DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile / DevOpsStatefulAgentProfile
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1951780Z Configures whether agents are stateless (fresh VM per job) or stateful (VM reused across jobs).
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1951784Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1952145Z Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html).
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1952218Z ```
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1952222Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1952450Z 3. **Examples** — replace the generic link with snippet placeholders:
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1952455Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1952530Z ```markdown
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1952604Z ## Examples
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1952706Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1952808Z ### Create a Managed DevOps Pool

2026-04-23T15:26:31.1952902Z ```C# Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool

2026-04-23T15:26:31.1953975Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1954073Z ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1954085Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1954494Z Add `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` tags to the existing sample files so `Update-Snippets.ps1` can inject code into the README placeholders.
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1954498Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1954903Z #### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1954906Z

2026-04-23T15:26:31.1955314Z 
2026-04-23T15:26:31.1955386Z ```csharp
